title: Best time to buy and sell stock III

content.problemStatement:
We are given an array Arr[] of length n. It represents the price of a stock on ‘n’ days. The following guidelines need to be followed:





We can buy and sell the stock any number of times.In order to sell the stock, we need to first buy it on the same or any previous day.We can’t buy a stock again after buying it once. In other words, we first buy a stock and then sell it. After selling we can buy and sell again. But we can’t sell before buying and can’t buy before selling any previously bought stock.We can do at most 2 transactions.



<
constraints: n is the number of days
content.inputFormat: The input is an array of integers representing the stock prices for each day.
content.outputFormat: The output is the maximum possible profit that can be achieved by buying and selling the stock at most twice.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use dynamic programming to keep track of the maximum profit after the first buy, first sell, second buy, and second sell.

code.Python3:
class StockProfit:
    def maxProfit(self, prices: list[int]) -> int:
        n = len(prices)
        if n < 2: return 0
        buy1, sell1, buy2, sell2 = -prices[0], 0, -prices[0], 0
        for i in range(1, n):
            buy1 = max(buy1, -prices[i])
            sell1 = max(sell1, buy1 + prices[i])
            buy2 = max(buy2, sell1 - prices[i])
            sell2 = max(sell2, buy2 + prices[i])
        return sell2
